name: Qt
description: Cross-platform C++/QML framework for building desktop, mobile, and embedded apps with native look and feel.

seo.title: Qt — No-code / Low-code App Builders
seo.meta_description: Qt: Cross-platform C++/QML framework for building desktop, mobile, and embedded apps with native look and feel.

when_to_use: Cross-platform desktop app targeting Windows, macOS, and Linux
Embedded UI for an industrial control panel using Qt/QML
Mobile and desktop app from a single C++ Qt codebase
High-performance native app replacing Electron for desktop
Custom UI toolkit for automotive infotainment system

pros: QML declarative UI is genuinely pleasant
Massive stdlib covers everything from HTTP to 3D
cons: LGPL compliance is a legal minefield
C++ learning curve is steep for web devs
Build toolchain feels ancient vs modern stacks
review.verdict: The serious choice for cross-platform native apps, but licensing and C++ tax make it a deliberate commitment.
review.best_for: Embedded, industrial, and desktop teams needing native C++ performance at scale
review.not_for: Web developers wanting quick cross-platform apps without C++ discipline

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Freemium with free tier; no peak-lock evidence
